name: checkbox-group
title: Checkbox Group
description: Coordinates a set of related checkboxes that share one value array (value / defaultValue / onValueChange / allValues), mirroring Base UI's checkbox-group contract while reusing the native Commons Checkbox for every 44px option row. Renders a fieldset with a legend as the group's accessible name, supports data-driven items or composed Checkbox children, and offers an optional parent select-all checkbox with a correct indeterminate (mixed) state. Inherits hint/error wiring, aria-invalid, and disabled from the Field contract.
useWhen: Selecting any number of options (zero, some, or all) from a related set
A multi-select filter list that benefits from a select-all shortcut
Grouping consent or preference checkboxes under one labelled question
avoidWhen: Choosing exactly one option from a set (use a radio-group)
A single yes/no choice (use one checkbox)
Toggling a setting that takes effect immediately (use a switch)
More than ~10 options where a searchable control fits better (use a combo-box or select)

keyboard: Tab moves focus to each checkbox in turn; Shift+Tab moves back
Space toggles the focused checkbox (including the select-all parent)
Toggling select-all ticks or unticks every enabled option at once
Disabling the group (native fieldset disabled) removes every option from the tab order
Clicking an option's label row toggles it (the label is part of the 44px target)
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default (each padded label row is the pointer target)
notes: The group is a fieldset role=group named by its legend, an explicit aria-labelledby, or a surrounding Field's label; each option is named by its own visible label via aria-labelledby.

The select-all parent carries the native indeterminate ('mixed') state when only some options are ticked, so its state is announced without relying on color; it exposes aria-controls listing the options it governs.
Select-all only toggles enabled options, preserving any disabled-and-ticked selections.

Each option keeps a visible border in every state (from the Commons Checkbox), so state survives Windows High Contrast / forced-colors mode; option descriptions are linked with aria-describedby and excluded from the accessible name.
Direction follows the DOM dir via CSS logical properties (native inputs), so RTL needs no extra provider.
